title: chamaesyce deltoidea subsp. serpyllum
intro: Chamaesyce deltoidea subsp. serpyllum, also known as the Spurred Spurge or Desert Spurge, is a succulent plant species native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations, wounds, and infections. The health benefits of Chamaesyce deltoidea subsp. serpyllum include its ability to reduce inflammation, promote wound healing, and support immune function. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, tinctures, or salves, and it is often used in folk medicine to treat a variety of ailments.
